key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal neural network can simultaneously predict penetration state, penetration depth, and weld seam morphology from weld pool images and welding parameters during laser welding.
engineering_relevance: The approach supports real-time in-situ quality monitoring and control for laser penetration welding, helping improve consistency and reduce defective welds.
method: Uses CMOS camera top-view weld pool images as the main sensing input.
Combines convolutional neural networks with state-space models to capture spatial and temporal welding features.
Trains a multi-task model to jointly classify penetration state, regress depth, and reconstruct cross-section morphology.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% weld cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the specific welding setup and dataset construction, so robustness across different materials, parameter ranges, and camera conditions should be validated before deployment.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
